prompt_text: Create a healthy weekly meal plan for a college student with a busy schedule who has only 20 minutes to cook per day.
response_text:
Here is a healthy, realistic weekly meal plan designed for a busy college student with a strict **20-minute cooking time** per day.

**The Core Philosophy:**
- **Batch Cook (Sunday):** You will spend about 1 hour on Sunday prepping ingredients. This is the secret to 20-minute weekdays.
- **No Waste:** Ingredients are reused across multiple meals (e.g., a bag of spinach, a rotisserie chicken).
- **Minimal Dishes:** Most meals are one-pan, one-pot, or no-cook.
- **Fuel for Brain & Body:** High in protein, fiber, and healthy fats to keep you full and focused.

**Equipment Needed:**
- Microwave
- Non-stick skillet or frying pan
- Sheet pan (for baking)
- Cutting board & knife
- Can opener

**Sunday Meal Prep (1 hour):**
1.  Cook 1 cup of **brown rice** or **quinoa** (makes ~3 cups).
2.  Pick the meat off **1 rotisserie chicken** (buy pre-cooked). Shred it.
3.  Wash and chop **bell peppers**, **onion**, and **broccoli**.
4.  Make a batch of **vinaigrette dressing** (3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp vinegar, salt, pepper).
5.  Hard-boil 4-6 eggs.

---

### The Weekly Meal Plan

| Day | Breakfast (5 min) | Lunch (10 min / no cook) | Dinner (20 min cook) | Snack Idea |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Mon** | Greek yogurt + granola + berries | **Chicken Salad Wraps**: Rotisserie chicken + Greek yogurt + grapes + whole wheat tortilla. | **Sheet Pan Salmon & Veggies**: Salmon fillet + broccoli + olive oil. Bake at 400°F for 15 min. | Apple + peanut butter |
| **Tue** | Hard-boiled egg + banana | **Leftover Salmon & Veggies** over pre-cooked rice. | **15-Minute Black Bean Quesadillas**: Canned black beans + cheese + salsa in a skillet. Serve with avocado. | Handful of almonds |
| **Wed** | Oatmeal (instant) + blueberries | **Leftover Quesadilla**. | **One-Pan Chicken & Veggies**: Pre-cooked chicken + pre-chopped bell peppers + pre-cooked rice + soy sauce. Stir-fry 5 min. | Greek yogurt |
| **Thu** | Smoothie (banana + spinach + milk + peanut butter) | **Tuna Salad Crackers**: Canned tuna + mayo + crackers + baby carrots. | **Microwave "Baked" Potato & Beans**: Microwave potato (5 min). Top with canned chili beans, cheese, and sour cream. | Hard-boiled egg |
| **Fri** | Greek yogurt + granola | **Leftover Chicken & Rice** (from Wed). | **20-Minute "Pantry" Pasta**: Cook pasta. While it boils, sauté garlic + canned tomatoes + spinach. Mix together. Top with parmesan. | Dark chocolate square |
| **Sat** | Scrambled eggs (2 eggs + spinach) | **Deconstructed Burrito Bowl**: Pre-cooked rice + canned corn + black beans + salsa + avocado. | **"Fancy" Ramen**: Instant ramen (discard flavor packet). Add soy sauce, soft-boiled egg, frozen edamame, and sriracha. | Cottage cheese + fruit |
| **Sun** | Smoothie | **Leftover Pasta**. | **Crustless Quiche**: Whisk 4 eggs + milk + leftover rotisserie chicken + frozen spinach. Bake in a skillet at 375°F for 18 min. | Popcorn |

---

### Grocery Shopping List (for the week)

**Produce:**
- Bananas (bunch)
- Berries (fresh or frozen)
- Apples
- Avocados (2-3)
- Spinach (1 bag)
- Broccoli (1 head)
- Bell peppers (2, any color)
- Onion (1)
- Garlic (1 head)
- Lemons or limes (2)
- Baby carrots (1 bag)
- Potatoes (2 large baking potatoes)
- Fresh or frozen edamame

**Protein & Dairy:**
- Rotisserie chicken (1 large)
- Salmon fillet (1, ~6 oz)
- Eggs (1 dozen)
- Greek yogurt (plain, large tub)
- Milk or plant-based milk
- Cheese (shredded cheddar or Mexican blend)
- Parmesan cheese (grated)
- Canned tuna (1 can)
- Canned black beans (2 cans)
- Canned chili beans (1 can)

**Pantry & Frozen:**
- Brown rice or quinoa (1 bag)
- Oats (1 container)
- Granola (1 bag)
- Whole wheat tortillas (1 pack)
- Pasta (1 box, penne or spaghetti)
- Instant ramen (1 pack, no seasoning)
- Canned diced tomatoes (1 can)
- Salsa (1 jar)
- Olive oil
- Vinegar (balsamic or apple cider)
- Soy sauce (low sodium)
- Peanut butter
- Almonds or mixed nuts
- Popcorn kernels or bagged popcorn
- Dark chocolate (1 bar)
- Frozen corn (1 bag)

### Tips for Success:
- **Use your microwave:** It's your best friend for steaming veggies (3 min) or reheating rice.
- **Don't skip prep:** The Sunday hour saves you 30+ minutes every single day.
- **Keep it simple:** It's okay to eat the same lunch twice. Variety comes from dinner.
- **Hydrate:** Keep a water bottle at your desk. Dehydration causes fatigue and cravings.
